Title: The regulation of zein biosynthesis in maize endosperm

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: seed storage protein; crucial for protein body initiation and kernel vitreousness in QPM
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: higher 27-kD γ-zein promotes protein body initiation and correlates with vitreous endosperm; duplication increases its expression in QPM
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi suppression; immunolocalization; genetic mapping; GWAS/linkage/map-based cloning
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 27-kD γ-zein is a structural storage protein required mainly for initiation of protein body formation. 2. Elevated accumulation strongly correlates with QPM kernel vitreousness. 3. Gene duplication of the 27-kD γ-zein locus underlies enhanced expression in QPM.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: developing endosperm; synthesized before α- and δ-zeins
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: rough endoplasmic reticulum-derived protein bodies, peripheral shell
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: reduced levels lower PB number and impair vitreousness
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not an enzyme or TF; included because review centrally summarizes its structural and nutritional significance.
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: O2; PBF1; OHP1; OHP2; ZmbZIP22; 50-kD γ-zein; 16-kD γ-zein; 15-kD β-zein
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: GWAS; linkage mapping; map-based cloning (accession NA)
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Overexpress or duplicate to restore vitreous endosperm in nutritionally improved opaque backgrounds.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major seed storage protein contributing to PB filling and morphology
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RNAi loss causes unusual budding PB structures; reduction can improve lysine/tryptophan indirectly by lowering zein proportion
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 22-kD α-zeins are major α-zein components encoded by z1C genes. 2. They help regulate PB morphology rather than initiation. 3. Their suppression creates abnormal budding PBs.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: reduction may impair PB morphology and kernel texture
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Interacts functionally with Fl1 and O10 for correct localization.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Selective reduction could improve essential amino acid balance, but may need 27-kD γ-zein support to preserve vitreousness.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major seed storage protein and principal PB filling component
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: suppression with 22-kD α-zeins reduces PB size without affecting PB number; reduction can elevate lysine/tryptophan proportion
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 19-kD α-zeins are the most abundant zein transcripts and proteins in maize endosperm. 2. They mainly function in PB filling. 3. Reducing their accumulation contributes to improved protein quality but can affect kernel texture.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Only select intact family members are expressed in a given inbred line.
Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: B73 has z1A/z1B/z1D genes with subset expressed
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Prime CRISPR targets for selective reduction of low-quality storage protein to improve lysine and tryptophan.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports ordered deposition of zeins in PBs
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Fl1 mutation mislocalizes 22-kD α-zein and causes opaque endosperm without major change in PB size or abundance
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis; interaction studies; localization analysis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Fl1 encodes a PB membrane protein required for proper 22-kD α-zein localization. 2. It interacts with 22-kD α-zein via DUF593. 3. Mutation causes opaque endosperm and altered internal PB organization.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: membrane protein with three transmembrane domains and DUF593
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Common gene because it mediates localization rather than enzymatic catalysis or transcriptional regulation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for maintaining PB organization when manipulating zein composition.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports ordered assembly of zeins in PBs
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: o10 mutation causes misshapen PBs by disrupting ring-shaped distribution of 22-kD α-zein and 16-kD γ-zein
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. O10 is an endosperm-specific PB-localized protein. 2. It colocalizes and interacts with 16-kD γ-zein and 22-kD α-zein. 3. Mutation disrupts PB morphology and ordered zein arrangement.
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: protein bodies at interface of α-core and γ-periphery
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Facilitates zein localization rather than directly altering transcription.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for preserving PB architecture in metabolic engineering of storage proteins.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports PB biogenesis and zein deposition
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: o1 mutant shows dilated ER and misshapen PBs, altering endosperm texture and zein deposition context
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. O1 encodes myosin XI-I associated with RER and PBs. 2. It is required for proper endomembrane trafficking during PB formation. 3. Mutation causes abnormal PB synthesis and opaque endosperm texture.
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: rough endoplasmic reticulum and protein bodies
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Transport/localization factor, not an enzyme or TF.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key for maintaining proper storage protein deposition and kernel texture.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: amino acid biosynthesis affecting zein translation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes proline biosynthesis from glutamic acid via glutamate-5-semialdehyde/Δ1-pyrroline-5-carboxylate pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major dietary/storage protein reserve in maize kernels
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: loss of Pro1 reduces zein biosynthesis and causes opaque endosperm through amino acid limitation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant characterization; metabolite/amino acid analysis; eIF2α phosphorylation analysis; protein profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Pro1 encodes Δ1-pyrroline-5-carboxylate synthetase required for proline biosynthesis. 2. Loss of Pro1 inhibits proline production, increases uncharged tRNApro and triggers eIF2α phosphorylation. 3. This causes general reduction in protein biosynthesis including zeins and leads to opaque endosperm.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: opaque endosperm; general reduction in protein biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene affects zein accumulation indirectly via amino acid supply rather than encoding zein itself.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Avoid loss-of-function in breeding; amino acid supply pathways are leverage points to maintain storage protein synthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: aromatic amino acid biosynthesis affecting zein translation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes conversion in tyrosine biosynthesis from arogenate to tyrosine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major dietary/storage protein reserve in maize kernels
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: mutation causes general reduction of zein accumulation and opaque endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. mto140 encodes ZmAroDH-1, an enzyme in tyrosine and phenylalanine biosynthesis. 2. mto140 mutant kernels show opaque endosperm and general reduction in zein storage proteins. 3. Amino acid limitation is inferred to repress zein protein biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: opaque endosperm; reduced storage protein accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review notes four maize AroDH family members; zmarodh-3 also causes opaque kernels.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful cautionary target; maintain aromatic amino acid biosynthesis to preserve zein accumulation.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: organic acid/amino acid metabolism affecting zein accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: activates substrate to corresponding acyl-CoA upstream of oxalyl-CoA metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major dietary/storage protein reserve in maize kernels
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: o7 mutation decreases zein concentration and causes opaque kernel phenotype via metabolic disruption
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. O7 encodes a defective acyl-CoA synthetase in opaque7 mutant. 2. o7 causes dramatic endosperm metabolome changes and reduced zein concentration. 3. The phenotype is linked to perturbed amino acid biosynthesis and opaque endosperm.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: opaque endosperm; altered metabolome; impaired nutritional quality
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts indirectly on zein by altering central metabolism and amino acid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Avoid disruptive alleles; central metabolism strongly conditions storage protein accumulation.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: organic acid/amino acid metabolism affecting zein accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: converts oxalyl-CoA into formyl-CoA and CO2
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major dietary/storage protein reserve in maize kernels
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: zmocd1 mutation reduces zein concentration and causes opaque endosperm via altered metabolome and amino acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmOCD1 encodes oxalyl-CoA decarboxylase downstream of O7. 2. zmocd1 mutant has opaque endosperm and major metabolic alterations. 3. Defects in amino acid biosynthesis are proposed to underlie decreased zein concentration.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: opaque endosperm; altered endosperm development and nutritional quality
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review links O7 and ZmOCD1 to α-ketoglutaric acid and oxaloacetic acid metabolism, indirectly influencing zein synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for maintaining metabolic homeostasis supporting storage protein deposition.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: zein biosynthesis; nutrient storage gene expression; carbon and amino acid metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: regulated by O11; activated by NKD1/NKD2
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: 19-kD α-zein genes; 22-kD α-zein genes; 27-kD γ-zein gene; 50-kD γ-zein gene; 10-kD δ-zein gene; 18-kD δ-zein gene; PPDK1; PPDK2; SSIII; GBF1; Mybr13; bZIP17; NKD2
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: directly activates almost all zein-coding genes except 16-kD γ-zein; also activates carbon/amino acid metabolism genes and TF genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: o2 reduces most zeins, increases non-zein proteins, raises lysine and tryptophan, and causes soft opaque endosperm
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis; transposon tagging; ChIP-seq; promoter binding assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. O2 is the first identified major transcriptional regulator of zein genes in developing endosperm. 2. It directly activates most zein-coding genes but not the 16-kD γ-zein gene. 3. Loss of O2 lowers zeins, increases lysine/tryptophan through proteome rebalancing, and affects broader nutrient metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: master regulator of zein accumulation and a central controller of endosperm nutrient storage
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major dietary/storage protein reserve in maize kernels
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: soft opaque endosperm despite improved protein quality
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: participates in TF cascade; mutually linked with PBF1, NKDs, and O11 in regulatory network
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: forms homo/heterodimeric regulatory complexes with OHP1/OHP2; interacts with PBF1 and ZmMADS47
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: ectopic O2/PBF1 can activate transgenic but not endogenous zein promoters; proposed promoter methylation differences at O2/PBF1 binding sites
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key locus for improving lysine/tryptophan through o2/QPM; requires modifier loci or 27-kD γ-zein enhancement to restore vitreous kernel texture.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review states O2 major for α-zein regulation, while OHPs are major for 27-kD γ-zein regulation.

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: 22-kD α-zein genes; 19-kD α-zein genes; 27-kD γ-zein gene; PPDK1; PPDK2; SSIII
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: binds prolamin box of many zein genes; strongest reported effect on 27-kD γ-zein and minor effect on 22-kD α-zein in RNAi lines
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PBF1 silencing severely reduces 27-kD γ-zein and slightly reduces 22-kD α-zein; also implicated in starch biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in vitro DNA binding; RNAi; interaction assays; promoter activation assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PBF1 binds the conserved prolamin box in many zein promoters. 2. RNAi indicates a severe requirement for 27-kD γ-zein accumulation and a smaller effect on 22-kD α-zein. 3. PBF1 also works with O2 in regulating starch biosynthetic genes.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: important endosperm-specific coordinator of storage protein and starch accumulation, especially influential for 27-kD γ-zein
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major dietary/storage protein reserve in maize kernels
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: silencing can impair zein accumulation and likely kernel quality
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: part of interconnected TF network with O2 and O11
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: interacts with O2, OHPs, and ZmbZIP22 in multiprotein regulatory assemblies
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: endogenous zein promoters may be inaccessible due to methylation at PBF1-binding regions
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for modulating both protein and starch accumulation; useful for understanding domestication and endosperm nutrient architecture.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific endosperm developmental role remains incompletely resolved because mutants were historically limited.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation via promoter binding and dimerization
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: binds O2-like box in 27-kD γ-zein promoter and promotes expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: silencing of OHPs significantly reduces 27-kD γ-zein abundance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: silencing; DNA binding assays; protein interaction assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OHP1 is an O2-heterodimerizing bZIP factor. 2. Together with OHP2 it binds the 27-kD γ-zein promoter. 3. OHP silencing lowers 27-kD γ-zein accumulation, indicating a major role in this branch.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major regulator for 27-kD γ-zein expression relative to O2
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports protein body initiation and kernel vitreousness in QPM context
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: forms homo/heterodimers with OHP2 and heterodimers with O2; interacts with PBF1 and ZmbZIP22 in regulatory complex
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulator to enhance 27-kD γ-zein and improve vitreousness while maintaining storage protein deposition.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review often treats OHP1 and OHP2 together; individual specificity not detailed.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation via promoter binding and dimerization
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: binds O2-like box in 27-kD γ-zein promoter and promotes expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: silencing of OHPs significantly reduces 27-kD γ-zein abundance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: silencing; DNA binding assays; protein interaction assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OHP2 is an O2-heterodimerizing bZIP factor. 2. Together with OHP1 it promotes 27-kD γ-zein expression. 3. OHP silencing demonstrates strong positive regulation of this zein.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major regulator for 27-kD γ-zein expression relative to O2
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports protein body initiation and kernel vitreousness in QPM context
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: forms homo/heterodimers with OHP1 and heterodimers with O2; interacts with PBF1 and ZmbZIP22 in regulatory complex
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target to enhance 27-kD γ-zein accumulation for endosperm modification.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review often treats OHP1 and OHP2 together; individual specificity not detailed.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: binds ACAGCTCA motif in the 27-kD γ-zein promoter and activates transcription
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: zmbzip22 mutant specifically reduces 27-kD γ-zein accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: DNA pull-down plus mass spectrometry; mutant analysis; promoter binding; LCI interaction assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmbZIP22 is an endosperm-specific activator identified from the 27-kD γ-zein promoter. 2. It directly binds the ACAGCTCA box. 3. Mutation specifically lowers 27-kD γ-zein accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: important specific activator of 27-kD γ-zein branch
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports protein body initiation and kernel vitreousness
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: interacts with PBF1 and OHPs, likely as part of a redundant TF complex
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to specifically boost 27-kD γ-zein for kernel texture improvement without broad pleiotropy.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Does not interact with O2 in LCI assay per review.

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: 19-kD α-zein genes; 22-kD α-zein genes; 50-kD γ-zein gene
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation in cooperation with O2
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates α-zein and 50-kD γ-zein genes largely through interaction with O2
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes accumulation of α-zeins and 50-kD γ-zein
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: protein interaction assays; promoter activation assays; Co-immunoprecipitation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmMADS47 is an O2-interacting MADS-box TF. 2. It controls activation of α-zein and 50-kD γ-zein genes. 3. Its transcriptional activity is enhanced through O2-mediated recruitment and interaction.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: co-activator strengthening zein promoter activation, especially α-zeins and 50-kD γ-zein
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major dietary/storage protein reserve in maize kernels
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be leveraged with O2 to strengthen zein expression in engineering contexts.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Constitutive expression contrasts with endosperm-specific O2.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: 16-kD γ-zein biosynthesis; carbohydrate and amino acid utilization; starch synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: directly binds 16-kD γ-zein promoter motif ACGCAA and positively regulates Bt2
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RNAi knockdown reduces 16-kD γ-zein and has pleiotropic effects on zein/non-zein proteins, starch synthesis, and kernel filling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi; direct promoter binding; expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmNAC128 is an endosperm-specific activator of the unique 16-kD γ-zein gene. 2. It acts redundantly with ZmNAC130 and also regulates Bt2. 3. Knockdown causes pleiotropic defects in carbohydrate/amino acid utilization and shrunken kernels.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: essential positive regulator for the O2/PBF1-independent 16-kD γ-zein branch
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports protein body expansion and storage protein deposition
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: pleiotropic defects; shrunken kernels; altered starch and protein accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important if specifically engineering 16-kD γ-zein and coordinating protein with starch accumulation; caution due to pleiotropy.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: 16-kD γ-zein promoter lacks prolamin box and O2 box.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: 16-kD γ-zein biosynthesis; carbohydrate and amino acid utilization; starch synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: directly binds 16-kD γ-zein promoter motif ACGCAA and positively regulates Bt2
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RNAi knockdown reduces 16-kD γ-zein and causes pleiotropic defects in storage metabolism and kernel development
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi; direct promoter binding; expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmNAC130 is an endosperm-specific NAC TF that redundantly activates the 16-kD γ-zein gene. 2. It also participates in starch synthesis via Bt2 regulation. 3. RNAi causes broad defects in protein and carbohydrate metabolism and shrunken kernels.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: essential positive regulator for the distinct 16-kD γ-zein branch
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports protein body expansion and storage protein deposition
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: pleiotropic defects; shrunken kernels; altered starch and protein accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for coordinating protein body expansion-related zein branch with starch metabolism, but pleiotropy must be managed.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: 16-kD γ-zein branch is independent of O2/PBF1 boxes.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: endosperm development and nutrient metabolism network upstream of zein regulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: directly regulates key TF genes involved in endosperm development and nutrient metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: acts as central hub coordinating upstream control of zein regulators and nutrient metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: regulatory network analysis as summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. O11 is a central hub in maize endosperm regulatory network. 2. It directly regulates O2 and PBF1 as well as NKD2 and ZmDOF3. 3. Through these downstream TFs it influences zein accumulation and nutrient metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: upstream network hub linking endosperm development to zein accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major dietary/storage protein reserve in maize kernels
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: network linkage with downstream TF cascades
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: O2 promoter; PBF1 promoter; NKD2 promoter; ZmDOF3 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Upstream target for coordinated nutrient-metabolism engineering, though likely pleiotropic.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core summarized regulator in this review network context.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: endosperm development and upstream regulation of zein network
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: aleurone and starchy endosperm as network context
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: directly activates O2 promoter and influences numerous endosperm genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: together with NKD2 coordinates endosperm development and indirectly zein accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant transcriptome analyses and promoter activation as summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NKD1 is an upstream TF in the endosperm network. 2. It directly activates O2 and Viviparous1. 3. nkd1 nkd2 mutants show thousands of DEGs, indicating broad control of nutrient accumulation programs.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: condition-setting upstream regulator of zein accumulation through O2 and broader endosperm developmental control
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major dietary/storage protein reserve in maize kernels
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Upstream developmental regulator; manipulation likely high impact but with strong pleiotropic risk.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review emphasizes combined nkd1 nkd2 effects rather than isolated roles.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: endosperm development and upstream regulation of zein network
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: aleurone and starchy endosperm as network context
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: directly activates O2 promoter and influences numerous endosperm genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: coordinates endosperm development and indirectly zein accumulation through O2-centered network
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant transcriptome analyses and promoter activation as summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NKD2 acts redundantly with NKD1 in maize endosperm. 2. It directly activates O2 and Viviparous1 and is itself regulated by O11. 3. It helps link developmental and nutrient accumulation networks affecting zein biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: upstream regulatory connector for zein biosynthesis network
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major dietary/storage protein reserve in maize kernels
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: network linkage among O11, NKD2, and O2
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential node for coordinated trait engineering but expected pleiotropic effects require caution.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified by review as part of mutual direct regulation network involving O2, PBF1, and O11.
